name: Windows Firewall Service unable to retrieve security policy
applicable_version: Windows Server 2008 and later; Windows Vista and later
definition: Windows Security Event ID 5027 records that the Windows Firewall Service could not retrieve security policy from local storage and continued enforcing the current policy.
trigger_scenarios: Microsoft states the event indicates low memory resources or Windows Firewall group policy registry corruption and often appears with Event ID 5028.

key_fields.explanation: The UInt32 error code for the retrieval failure. Microsoft examples show ErrorCode 2147942413.
The host whose firewall service could not read local policy. Internet-facing and remote-access systems should be prioritized.
Microsoft states 5027 typically occurs with 5028 when the service cannot parse the new security policy.

false_positives: Low memory, registry corruption, or Group Policy misconfiguration can cause the error without malicious activity.
Broken firewall policy deployments may generate 5027 across many hosts at the same time.

attck_mapping.technique_name: Disable or Modify System Firewall: Windows Host Firewall

detection_notes: Microsoft says 5027 is usually configuration-related, so escalate to T1686.003 when threshold=1 occurrence is paired with firewall policy changes, 5028 parse failures, or 5030/5035 service-start failures on the same host. ErrorCode 2147942413 is the Microsoft example and should be retained for troubleshooting; the attack signal is that the Windows Firewall Service cannot retrieve local policy, potentially leaving an older or attacker-favorable policy enforced while remote access rules are changed elsewhere.
kql_snippet:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 5027
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, ErrorCode
sample_log:
The Windows Firewall Service was unable to retrieve the security policy from the local storage.
ErrorCode: 2147942413
Computer: WEB01

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Security Event ID 5027
queries.query:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 5027
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, EventData
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Windows Security Events connector populates SecurityEvent.
Map raw XML fields to the event-specific fields documented on this page.

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Security Event ID 5027
queries.query:
index=wineventlog source="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=5027
| table _time, host, user, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index, source and field aliases vary by deployment.
Apply event-specific field conditions after validating local extraction.

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Security Event ID 5027
assumptions: The applicable Windows event channel is ingested into Cortex XDR.
Inspect raw provider fields because parsed names vary by collection path.

queries.title: Windows Security Event ID 5027
queries.query:
title: Windows Security Event ID 5027
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: security
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 5027
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This rule retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.
Add the documented conditions and correlations in the destination SIEM.

value_references.meaning: The error-code example in Microsoft's event reference; retain the actual code for troubleshooting.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret ErrorCode with the complete event and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

value_references.meaning: Windows Firewall Service could not retrieve its security policy.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret EventID with the complete event and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

faqs.question: What does Windows Event ID 5027 mean?
faqs.answer: Windows Firewall Service could not retrieve its security policy.

faqs.question: Does Event ID 5027 always indicate malicious activity?
faqs.answer: No. Microsoft describes this as commonly configuration-related.

faqs.question: How should Event ID 5027 be investigated?
faqs.answer: Decode ErrorCode and correlate with firewall policy changes, service failures and the last successfully applied policy.

sources.title: 5027: Windows Firewall Service unable to retrieve security policy
Disable or Modify System Firewall: Windows Host Firewall (T1686.003)
